versionNotes.1.21.4: Latest stable release. NeoForge is the recommended Forge-like loader.
versionNotes.1.21.1: Stable. Both Fabric and NeoForge have good mod coverage.
versionNotes.1.20.1: The most popular modded version. Enormous Fabric and Forge/NeoForge ecosystems. Recommended for most modpacks.
versionNotes.1.19.2: Second most popular. Large mod ecosystem on both Fabric and Forge.
versionNotes.1.18.2: Good coverage. Many classic mods still actively supported.
versionNotes.1.16.5: Legacy popular version with Create and many tech mods.
versionNotes.1.12.2: Classic legacy version. Many old Forge mods, but no longer actively developed.
versionNotes.1.8.9: Used mainly for PvP clients like Lunar Client. Very few modern mods.

Fabric.description: Modern, lightweight modloader. Fast startup, great for optimization mods. Requires Fabric API as a dependency.
Fabric.notes: Sodium, Lithium, and most optimization mods are Fabric-only.

Forge.description: The original Minecraft modloader. The largest legacy mod ecosystem. Slower startup.
Forge.notes: For 1.20.1+, NeoForge is increasingly preferred as the active Forge fork.

NeoForge.description: An actively maintained fork of Forge for Minecraft 1.20.1 and newer. The modern choice for Forge-style modding.
NeoForge.notes: Most Forge mods for 1.20.1+ are compatible with NeoForge.

Quilt.description: A fork of Fabric with extended features and a compatibility layer for Fabric mods.

Vanilla.description: Plain Minecraft without any modloader. Supports only resource packs and data packs.
Vanilla.notes: No mods can be installed on Vanilla installations.

serverTypes.Vanilla: Official Mojang server software. No plugins, no mods. Best for small friend groups.
serverTypes.Paper: Highly optimized Bukkit/Spigot fork. Recommended for most servers. Supports Bukkit plugins.
serverTypes.Purpur: Paper fork with extra configuration options and quality-of-life features.
serverTypes.Fabric: Fabric modded server. Requires Fabric API. Server mods must be server-side compatible.
serverTypes.Forge: Forge modded server. All connected clients must have the same mods installed.
serverTypes.NeoForge: NeoForge modded server for 1.20.1+. Clients need matching NeoForge mods.
serverTypes.Spigot: Plugin-compatible server. The base for Paper. Slower than Paper, use Paper instead.
serverTypes.Bukkit: The original plugin server. Now mostly superseded by Spigot and Paper.

commonMistakes: Mixing Fabric and Forge mods in the same installation — they are incompatible
Installing a mod designed for a different Minecraft version
Forgetting to install Fabric API when using Fabric mods
Using Sodium on Forge instead of Embeddium (the Forge port)
Trying to install server-only mods on a client installation

Searching Modrinth for a Forge mod with loader=NeoForge, or vice versa — Forge and NeoForge are DISTINCT loaders on Modrinth. Always use the exact loader the user chose. Never substitute one for the other.
